# Copilot Instructions — Tide & Fortune

## Project Overview
A 2D isometric sailing game built in Rust using Bevy. Age of sail setting, Caribbean-inspired archipelago. Solo sailing focus with island exploration, treasure hunting, naval combat, sailing with NPCs, and select fleet battle moments. Will include ship upgrade and customisation, as well as "tier" jumps in user progression that unlock new mechanics and content, as well as a new class of player ship. 

The design of this game is captured in `docs/design/GAME_DESIGN.md` — please refer to that for any design questions. If you find yourself needing to make design decisions that aren't covered there, please update the document with your decision and rationale.

## Rust & Bevy Conventions
- Bevy version: 0.15 (check Cargo.toml before assuming APIs)
- Use `#[derive(Component, Bundle, Resource, Event)]` appropriately — never
  store game state in plain structs outside ECS unless in `tf_core`
- Prefer `Query` filters over fetching everything and filtering in Rust code
- Systems should do one thing; compose behaviour through system ordering and
  events, not monolithic functions
- Use `EventWriter` / `EventReader` for cross-system communication
- Avoid `.unwrap()` in systems; use `if let` or `let Ok(...)` patterns
- All game logic (physics, economy, narrative) lives in `tf_core` with zero
  Bevy dependencies — pure Rust, fully testable
- `tf_simulation` depends on `tf_core` and Bevy ECS only — no rendering
- `tf_render` depends on `tf_simulation` — rendering only, no logic
- Never put logic in render systems

## Architecture Rules
- One file per major system (sailing_physics.rs, wind_field.rs, etc.)
- Pub API of each crate defined in lib.rs with explicit `pub use`
- No circular dependencies between crates — see docs/architecture/CRATE_CONTRACTS.md
- Feature flags: `multiplayer` (off by default), `dev_tools` (enables debug overlays)

## ECS Patterns to Follow
- Use marker components (`struct PlayerShip;`) liberally for query filtering
- Bundles for spawning — never spawn components piecemeal outside of a Bundle
- Prefer `Changed<T>` and `Added<T>` filters to avoid redundant work
- State gating: `.run_if(in_state(GameMode::Sailing))` — see GameMode enum
- Run `check_anchored_ship_safety` and `update_wind_field` in every GameMode

## Sailing Physics (Core Mechanic — get this right)
- Wind is a 2D vector field (Resource: WindField), sampled at ship position
- Apparent wind = true_wind − ship_velocity
- Drive force derived from angle of attack on sails and effective sail area
- In Tier 1 user progression: auto sail trim — the physics run fully but trim_efficiency is
  computed automatically at optimal for current point of sail
- Points of sail: InIrons, CloseHauled, CloseReach, BeamReach,
  BroadReach, Running — derive from angle between apparent wind and heading
- Gybe warning system triggers before crossing dead downwind

## Game Modes
```rust
enum GameMode { Sailing, Anchoring, OnFoot, Combat, PortInterior,
                FleetBattle, Cutscene }
```
All systems must declare which modes they run in. See docs/architecture/ECS_PATTERNS.md.

## Code Style
- `snake_case` for everything except types and traits
- Doc comments on all public items (`///`)
- Unit tests in the same file for `tf_core` logic (sailing math especially)
- Integration tests in `tests/` at crate root
- No `unwrap`, no `clone` without a comment explaining why

## Asset Conventions
- Sprites: `/assets/sprites/{category}/{name}.png`
- Tilemaps: `/assets/tilemaps/{name}.ron`
- Audio: `/assets/audio/{category}/{name}.ogg`
- All asset paths defined as constants in `tf_render::assets`

## What NOT to do
- Do not add dependencies without checking existing Cargo.toml first
- Do not implement networking until the `multiplayer` feature issue is opened
- Do not use `bevy_rapier` — sailing physics uses a custom integrator
- Do not break the tf_core / tf_simulation / tf_render layering
- Do not add placeholder `todo!()` without a linked GitHub issue number

## Other Instructions
- If responding to the prompt requires archictural changes or large refactors, please ensure `.copilot-instructions.md` and `docs/design/GAME_DESIGN.md` are updated, if relevant.
